technology_stacks_used_for_parsing.name: .NET / C#
technology_stacks_used_for_parsing.parser_strategy: compiler/AST-backed parser when available; otherwise C# source structural parser; project XML parsed structurally

ast_backends.name: Roslyn compiler AST
ast_backends.parser_strategy: available for future C# semantic extraction; not active until a Roslyn semantic extractor is implemented

ast_backends.name: tree-sitter C# AST
ast_backends.parser_strategy: C# syntax tree parser for class/interface/record/struct declarations; framework extractors still handle routes, DI, and call candidates
ast_backends.reason_unavailable: tree_sitter and tree_sitter_c_sharp Python packages are not both installed

technology_stacks_used_for_parsing.name: Entity Framework Core
technology_stacks_used_for_parsing.parser_strategy: DbContext, IEntityTypeConfiguration, AddDbContext, repository usage extraction

limitations: C# symbol extraction uses static structural/framework parsing unless an optional Roslyn or tree-sitter backend is available.
Method-call extraction records dependency candidates, not complete call graphs; overload resolution and dynamic dispatch are not resolved.
Razor/Blazor parsing captures directives and injected services but does not compile generated partial classes.
Module guesses are evidence-derived domain/project/support candidates for parsed facts only; final module maps still require evidence-pack consolidation and human review.
Route templates with framework conventions are captured as templates; runtime route token transformers and filters are not fully evaluated.
